project_overview.project_name: Ethiopia Renewable Energy Guarantees Program (REGREP) - Phase I
project_overview.project_development_objective: Increase renewable energy generation capacity through private sector participation in Ethiopia
project_overview.beneficiaries: Electricity consumers in Ethiopia and the region, EEP, IPP investors and lenders

geographic_scope.fragile_or_conflict_affected_areas: no - though Ethiopia is located in the conflict-affected Horn of Africa region

key_components.description: 100 MW solar PV power plant developed by EGP-led consortium covering 250 ha, generating approximately 280 GWh per year under 20-year PPA

implementation_arrangements.coordination_mechanisms: PPP Directorate General at Ministry of Finance oversees PPP transactions; IPP Unit at EEP manages implementation
implementation_arrangements.procurement_approach: Competitive bidding process under PPP Proclamation framework with open international competitive bidding as default
implementation_arrangements.monitoring_and_evaluation: EEP consolidates progress reports from IPP project companies and reports to IDA; quarterly monitoring of key indicators

identified_risks.risk_description: Tensions between Government and civil society could negatively affect IPP preparation and implementation due to perceived social risks and land allocation issues
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Community engagement throughout project development under ESMF and RPF; comprehensive communications strategy

identified_risks.risk_description: High current account deficit and dwindling currency reserves present risks for foreign currency availability and exchange rate volatility
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: DPO series addressing structural measures; commercial structure ensuring appropriate risk allocation; new tariff framework with exchange rate pass-through

identified_risks.risk_description: EEP and MoF have limited experience in IPP transactions requiring specialized technical, legal and financial skills
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Transaction advisers and technical assistance provided by various donors; capacity building support through PPIAF grants and IDA Credits

identified_risks.risk_description: Utilities' financial viability challenges and inadequacy of current policy framework to support IPP development
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Power sector reform program under DPO series including tariff reforms, debt restructuring, and operational efficiency improvements

identified_risks.risk_description: Take-or-pay contracts introduce risk of deemed generation payments whether energy is dispatched or not
identified_risks.mitigation_measures: Improvements to variable renewable energy integration; upgraded system planning tools; move toward competitive wholesale market

safeguards_and_social.grievance_redress_mechanism: yes - Project GRM established with community-based systems through Kebele leadership and council of elders, with judicial system as last resort
safeguards_and_social.gender_considerations: Gender Action Plan implementation; employment opportunities for women; gender equality in resettlement compensation; GBV prevention mechanisms

fcv_relevant_details.conflict_sensitivity: Project located in Horn of Africa region with history of conflict; peace agreement with Eritrea signed; attention to community tensions and land allocation issues
fcv_relevant_details.fragility_context: Ethiopia experiencing unprecedented political and economic change with new Prime Minister; previous political and social crises requiring states of emergency
fcv_relevant_details.security_considerations: Site security managed by private security firm with trained, unarmed personnel; security arrangements following Voluntary Principles on Security and Human Rights
fcv_relevant_details.displacement_or_refugees: yes - 38 households physically displaced, 562 total project-affected households requiring resettlement and livelihood restoration
